gates_re_evaluated.rationale: No state/coordinator/build-complete.json exists in this run. The run pre-dates the v1.2 explicit handoff signal.

decisions/technical-discovery/sections-v1.json does not include machine_checkable_assertions[] on its inflection_resolutions[]. Critic's charter-implementation conformance check would have nothing to verify.
Sections file lacks acceptance_assertions[]; prose acceptance criteria are not derivable into structured tests.
Verification report's checks are all static inspection (grep + structural). No artifact-exercise pass, no DOM-capable sandbox loading. Behavioral defects could not have been caught.

No inline deviations documented, but no state/inline-deviations/ directory exists either. Cannot determine whether deviations occurred during build; pattern of skipped logging predates the requirement.

Original verification did not run under any DOM-capable sandbox at all (was static inspection only). Production fidelity is moot when behavioral exercise was never attempted. Compounded: tic-tac-toe has no runtime deps to substitute, so the failure is the absence of exercise rather than substitution per se.
No PNV exists. The prompt's named verb 'play tic-tac-toe' is never asserted end-to-end against the deliverable.
No TD-introduced IPs in this run; both IPs were Discovery-sourced. Schema absence is non-blocking.
output/final/ matches output/integration/ byte-for-byte (no recovery patches). Divergence record not required.
Neither history/build-summary.md nor history/decision-index.json exists. Required for delivery under v1.6.
TD did not enumerate candidate verbs or pick the load-bearing one. Coupled with PNV absence, the verification regime never tested the prompt's central job.

incidental_observations: The artifact almost certainly does play tic-tac-toe correctly — the rules engine and UI from inspection look sound. The reclassification is about verification regime conformance, not artifact correctness.
Reflects the v1.0 era of the architecture: static inspection sufficed for the first run because the prompt's domain is small and the architecture's verification gates hadn't been built yet.
Useful as a baseline of how much the architecture has grown: 7 gates fail not because the build is bad but because the gates didn't exist when the build happened.

recovery_options.description: Add machine_checkable_assertions to sections, add acceptance_assertions, write a CV artifact-exercise runner that loads index.html in jsdom-with-real-resource-loading, derive PNV ('place an X in any cell, observe X rendered in that cell'), produce Historian artifacts, write divergence record (none) and verification report under v1.6 gates.

Discard prior outputs and re-run the prompt 'build me a single-page tic-tac-toe game where I play against the computer' under the current v1.6 architecture. Cleaner; produces a v1.6-native run with all required artifacts in place from the start.

recommendation_rationale: The artifact appears correct from static inspection; rebuilding is wasteful when the gates that would be added are documentation/structuring rather than functional changes. Patching adds the missing artifacts (assertions, PNV, Historian outputs, exercise runner) without disturbing the working code. Estimated effort: 1 dispatch each of TD-amendment, edge-case-testing-rerun, CV-rerun, Historian. A rebuild would re-do the entire build for marginal benefit.
